bibliographic_data.title: Reducing Gender Bias in Abusive Language Detection
bibliographic_data.abstract: Abusive language detection models tend to have a problem of being biased toward identity words of a certain group of people because of imbalanced training datasets. For example, 'You are a good woman' was considered 'sexist' when trained on an existing dataset. Such model bias is an obstacle for models to be robust enough for practical use. In this work, we measure gender biases on models trained with different abusive language datasets, while analyzing the effect of different pre-trained word embeddings and model architectures. We also experiment with three bias mitigation methods: (1) debiased word embeddings, (2) gender swap data augmentation, and (3) fine-tuning with a larger corpus. These methods can effectively reduce gender bias by 90-98% and can be extended to correct model bias in other scenarios.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes three methods for reducing gender bias in abusive language detection models: debiased word embeddings, gender swap data augmentation, and fine-tuning with a larger corpus.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors measure gender biases using an unbiased test set generated by the identity term template method. They experiment with different pre-trained word embeddings and model architectures to mitigate bias.
outcomes: Reduced gender bias in abusive language detection models by 90-98%
Proposed three methods for mitigating gender bias
core_content.full_text_markdown: Automatic detection of abusive language is crucial due to its potential to cause personal trauma, cyber-bullying, and discrimination. However, existing models can be biased towards certain groups, such as considering 'You are a good woman' sexist. This paper addresses the issue by measuring gender biases in different datasets and proposing three mitigation methods: debiased word embeddings, gender swap data augmentation, and fine-tuning with a larger corpus. The authors use an unbiased test set to evaluate these methods effectively.
